description.description: Name or description of the route.
description.title: Description

parentchgsstatus.description: Checkbox that determines if the status on child work orders gets updated automatically when you change the status of the parent work order.
parentchgsstatus.title: Route Stops Inherit Status Changes

routestopsbecome.description: Identifies what Route Stops become when Routes are applied to Work Orders - Child Work Orders, Work Order Tasks or Entries in the Multi Asset, Location & CI table
routestopsbecome.title: When this Route is applied to a Work Order, the Route Stops will become

route.description: Identifier of the route. This field must be unique.
route.title: Route

assetfeatureid.description: Asset Feature that is the focus of work (e.g. Guardrail 127) as opposed to the start/end asset features that identify where the work takes place
assetfeatureid.title: Asset Feature ID

startassetfeatureid.description: A previously defined feature or relationship used, in conjunction with the start offset, to determine the start measure for this linear segment.
startassetfeatureid.title: Start Feature ID

jpnum.description: Specify the job plan to be used on the work order generated for this route stop. If this route is used on work orders generated from PMs, the job plan is copied from this field if it is filled in; otherwise it is copied from the PM.
jpnum.title: Job Plan

assetlocsiteid.description: The Site to which the asset or location belongs to.
assetlocsiteid.title: Site

startmeasure.description: Absolute distance taken from the start of the linear asset to where this route stop is initially encountered.  This can be manually entered or calculated by entering a start reference point and start offset.
startmeasure.title: Start Measure

endoffsetunitid.description: Unit of measure for the offset (before or after) from the end reference point of the asset on this route stop.
endoffsetunitid.title: Unit of End Offset

routestopid.description: Identifies a stop record added to a route.
routestopid.title: Stop Added to Route

assetlocorgid.description: The Organization to which the asset or locations belongs to.
assetlocorgid.title: Organization

endzoffset.description: Distance above or below this linear asset.  For example, if a sign is 10 feet above the road, the  Z-Offset is 10 feet.  This value is measured from the Z  Reference Point and can be positive (above) or negative (below).
endzoffset.title: End Z Offset

totalworkunits.description: Total Work Units, for all assets
totalworkunits.title: Total Work Units

endbasemeasure.description: An absolute measure that is calculated by converting from the end measure using a defined conversion method.  Conversion methods are defined in the Assets application using the Add/Modify Conversions action.
endbasemeasure.title: End Base Measure

startoffsetunitid.description: Unit of measure for the offset (before or after) from the start reference point of the asset on this route stop.
startoffsetunitid.title: Unit of Start Offset

startoffset.description: Distance used in conjunction with the start reference point to determine where the route stop begins.
startoffset.title: Start Offset

endassetfeatureid.description: A previously defined feature or relationship used, in conjunction with the end offset to determine the end measure for this linear segment.
endassetfeatureid.title: End Asset Feature ID

description.description: Name or description of the route stop. (WS) (custom - length increased to 150 to handle asset.description length increase).
description.title: Description

endoffset.description: Distance used in conjunction with the end reference point to determine where the route stop ends.
endoffset.title: End Offset

assetnum.description: Identifier of the asset at this route stop. You can specify an asset or a location identifier, but not both, for the stop.
assetnum.title: Asset

endyoffsetref.description: Reference point from which the Y offset is measured
endyoffsetref.title: End Y Offset Referent

startzoffset.description: Distance above or below this linear asset.  For example, if a sign is 10 feet above the road, the  Z-Offset is 10 feet.  This value is measured from the Z  Reference Point and can be positive (above) or negative (below).
startzoffset.title: Start Z Offset

startzoffsetref.description: Reference point from which the start Z-Offset (distance above or below the linear asset) is measured.
startzoffsetref.title: Start Z Offset Referent

startyoffsetref.description: Reference point from which the Y offset is measured
startyoffsetref.title: Start Y Offset Referent

stopsequence.description: Sequence number of this route stop. This field can be a positive integer or null.
stopsequence.title: Sequence

basemeasureunitid.description: Usually, this is the same as the unit of measure  for the asset. If they differ, Maximo will display measures in the unit of measure but will store measures in both the unit of measure and base unit of measure.  A conversion between the unit of measure and base unit of measure, entered via the Units of Measure and Conversion Action in Assets, must exist if the base unit of measure differs from the unit of measure.
basemeasureunitid.title: Unit of Base Measure

endmeasure.description: Absolute distance taken from the start of the linear asset to where this route stop ends.  This can be manually entered or calculated by entering an end reference point and end offset.
endmeasure.title: End Measure

startyoffset.description: Perpendicular distance from this linear asset.  For example, if a sign is 10 feet to the right of the road, the Y-Offset is 10 feet.  This value is measured from the Y Reference Point and can be positive (right) or negative (left).
startyoffset.title: Start Y Offset

endyoffset.description: Perpendicular distance from this linear asset.  For example, if a sign is 10 feet to the right of the road, the Y-Offset is 10 feet.  This value is measured from the Y Reference Point and can be positive (right) or negative (left).
endyoffset.title: End Y Offset

location.description: Location identifier of this route stop. You can specify either a location or an asset identifier for the stop, but not both.
location.title: Location

inspformnum.description: The number of the inspection form.
inspformnum.title: Inspection Form

startbasemeasure.description: An absolute measure that is calculated by converting from the end measure using a defined conversion method.  Conversion methods are defined in the Assets application using the Add/Modify Conversions action.
startbasemeasure.title: Start Base Measure

endzoffsetref.description: Point from which the Z-Offset is measured.
endzoffsetref.title: End Z Offset Referent

401.description:
BMXAA0033I Your session timed out due to inactivity.
BMXAA0026E The action could not be performed because the application server security is enabled. Proper authorization is required to perform this action. Report this error to your system administrator.
BMXAA7901E You cannot log in at this time. Contact the system administrator.
BMXAA0025E The method cannot be called because application server security is not enabled. Enable Application Server Security. For details, see information on defining server security in the information center.
BMXAA4122E You cannot be connected to an application server on the specified host. Ensure that an application server is running.
BMXAA0020E The session that is used to access business objects is either not valid or has timed out. Verify that the remote system is avaialble, then log in and authenticate again.
BMXAA0021E User name and password combination are not valid. Try again.
BMXAA0011E User {0} is inactive. Specify a different user.

412.description: BMXAA8229W Record {0} has been updated by another user. Your changes have not been saved. Refresh the record and try again.

413.description: BMXAA2249E The file being attached exceeds the size limit of {0}MB. See your system administrator to have the limit adjusted.

403.description:
BMXAA1671I Skip transaction.
BMXAA2283E Your password has expired.

404.description:
BMXAA4216E Unknown Object named {0} has been encountered. Gather the logs from the directory and determine the cause and source of the error.
BMXAA9510E The stable page resource was not found because the reload of a stable page was attempted or the idle expiry time has expired.
BMXAA4187E The relationship {0} does not exist for business object {1}. Verify that the relationship specified in parameter {0} exists in the Maxrelationship table.
BMXAA1283E The {0} value in the Consumed By field cannot be used. Define an object structure with a Consumed By value that is valid for the processing action.
BMXAA8727E The OSLC resource {0} with the ID {1} was not found as it does not exist in the system. In the database, verify whether the resource for the ID exists.

415.description: BMXAA8823E The format {0} is not supported. You must use a supported format such as application/json.

405.description: BMXAA8815E The HTTP method DELETE is not permitted on a collection resource. Only HTTP GET and POST methods are supported on collection URIs.

409.description: BMXAA9524E The transaction ID {1} already exists in the OSLC transaction table with resource ID {2} for object structure {3}.

opmodeor.name: opmodeor
opmodeor.description: if set to 1, this will cause the oslc.where clause to set the query mode to OR clause instead of AND.

oslc.properties.name: oslc.properties
oslc.properties.description: Select clause for selecting a partial view of the member resource (like an individual workorder).

transactionid.name: transactionid
transactionid.description: This is used to prevent de-duplication for create/update/delete. If this header is present then the value is compared to a list of committed transaction ids.If it has a match, then it is considered as a duplicate transaction and an HTTP conflict error is generated.

oslc.where.name: oslc.where
oslc.where.description: Where clause to filter the set of resources

x-allow-events.name: x-allow-events
x-allow-events.description: For a create/update/delete, if the request wants to suppress the object events, this request header needs to be set to 0. By default all events are fired unless programmatically stopped.

x-method-override.name: x-method-override
x-method-override.description: Due to constraints from browser the HTTP POST method is re-used for creates and updates and deletes.This request header is used to convey the specifics of the POST request. PATCH is used for update. DELETE is used for deleting the resource. BULK is used for supporting bulk operations.If this header is not present, the POST request is treated as a create request.

gbrange.name: gbrange
gbrange.description: The range clause for the group by query.

lean.name: lean
lean.description: If set to 1, this will instruct the api to work with the "lean" json format as opposed to the namespaced json that it produces by default. This option is supported only starting 7601 release of the Maximo base. Its preferred to use this format as it reduces the size of the json payload.

oslc.select.name: oslc.select
oslc.select.description:
Filter clause following the OSLC query syntax
(see below for more details on this)

gbcols.name: gbcols
gbcols.description: The comma separated group by columns.

content-type.name: content-type
content-type.description: The mime-type of the request document.

gbsortby.name: gbsortby
gbsortby.description: The order by clause for the group by query. Follows the same syntax as oslc.orderBy.

id.name: id
id.description: Unique id for object structure resource

collectioncount.name: collectioncount
collectioncount.description: If set to 1, it will instruct the api to include the total count of records that matched the query ( responseInfo.totalCount). By default it will not return that unless the maximo property mxe.oslc.collectioncount is set to 1. This is a recommended way to control collection count at the request level.

oslc.searchTerms.name: oslc.searchTerms
oslc.searchTerms.description: Record wide text search for tokens specified as this query parameter value. Example oslc.searchTerms="pump","broken"

oslc.paging.name: oslc.paging
oslc.paging.description: Instruct the server to page the query response based on the default page size. The default page size needs to be configured on the server side for this to take effect.

apikey.name: apikey
apikey.description: Set to the users apikey. This is used when the 'api' context is used (instead of 'oslc'). Do make sure that the apikey is generated for the user. You can use the Administration app to generate an apikey

oslc.pageSize.name: oslc.pageSize
oslc.pageSize.description: Instruct the server to page the query result with the specified page size.

stablepaging.name: stablepaging
stablepaging.description: If set to true, will instruct the server to page the resource from a in session MboSet.

accept.name: accept
accept.description: The mime-type of the accept document.

patchtype.name: patchtype
patchtype.description: This request header with the value of MERGE will instruct the server to treat a PATCH request (POST with x-method-override as PATCH) processor to merge the child objects (array of child objects) in the request with the corresponding child elements in the server. If this header is not present, the server will delete the child objects that are missing from the request.

properties.name: properties
properties.description: Select clause for selecting a partial view of the member resource.

get.description: Get a MXAPIROUTES based on the id {id}

tags: Maximo API for Job Plans (MXAPIJOBPLAN) POST Actions

get.description: Get a (paged) collection of MXAPIJOBPLAN based on the where filter (oslc.where) and select clause (oslc.select)
Get a (paged) collection of MXAPIROUTES based on the where filter (oslc.where) and select clause (oslc.select)
